_doc: VORA — Single source of truth for body-type criteria. EDIT THIS FILE to tune how body types are decided. It is read by BOTH the deterministic rule engine (lib/body-type-rules.ts) AND the Groq vision prompt (lib/groq-classify.ts), so measurements and photos always use the same criteria. The engine — not the LLM — makes the final decision, so results stay exact and reproducible.

thresholds._doc: All knobs that decide a body type. Percentages compare the upper body (broader of shoulders/bust) against the hips. Ratios are waist ÷ hips.
thresholds._balanceTolerancePct: If the upper body and hips differ by LESS than this %, the frame is 'balanced' and the waist decides the type. At or above it, the wider half wins (pear or inverted-triangle).
thresholds._strongDominancePct: Upper/hip difference at or above this % counts as a clear, high-confidence dominance.
thresholds._waistDefinedRatio: On a balanced frame, waist/hip AT OR BELOW this = clearly defined waist → hourglass.
thresholds._waistFullRatio: On a balanced frame, waist/hip AT OR ABOVE this = little waist definition → apple. Between the two ratios → rectangle.

confidence._doc: How far the deciding value must clear its threshold for 'high' vs 'medium' confidence. Below mediumMargin → 'low'.

hourglass.label: Hourglass
hourglass.measurementRule: Upper body and hips are balanced (within balanceTolerancePct) AND waist/hip ≤ waistDefinedRatio.
hourglass.promptDefinition: Shoulders/upper torso and hips appear visually balanced, and the waist is clearly narrower than both. Strong waist definition with balanced upper and lower proportions.

rectangle.label: Rectangle
rectangle.measurementRule: Upper body and hips are balanced (within balanceTolerancePct) AND waistDefinedRatio < waist/hip < waistFullRatio.
rectangle.promptDefinition: Shoulders/upper torso, waist, and hips appear similar in width, with minimal waist definition. The silhouette is straight and balanced, without strong upper or lower dominance.

pear.label: Pear
pear.measurementRule: Hips exceed the upper body by at least balanceTolerancePct.
pear.promptDefinition: Hips appear clearly wider than shoulders/upper torso. The lower body visually dominates the silhouette.

apple.label: Apple
apple.measurementRule: Upper body and hips are balanced (within balanceTolerancePct) AND waist/hip ≥ waistFullRatio (little waist definition).
apple.promptDefinition: The midsection/torso appears visually dominant and the waist is not clearly defined; the waist is close in width to the bust/upper torso or hips. Judge by proportion and waist definition only, never by weight.

inverted-triangle.label: Inverted Triangle
inverted-triangle.measurementRule: Upper body exceeds the hips by at least balanceTolerancePct.
inverted-triangle.promptDefinition: Shoulders/upper torso appear clearly wider than hips. The upper body visually dominates the silhouette and hips appear narrower.

_decisionOrder: Evaluation priority. A clearly dominant half (pear/inverted-triangle) is decided before waist-based types. This order is structural and lives in code; tune the numbers above to change outcomes.

photo._doc: Guidance for the Groq vision model. The model ONLY estimates the visual evidence below — it never picks the final type. The engine maps the evidence to a type using classifyFromVisualEvidence().

confidenceCriteria.high: Full-body photo(s), neutral pose, fitted clothing, good lighting, clear shoulder/waist/hip silhouette, front view available.
confidenceCriteria.medium: Mostly usable photo(s), minor pose/clothing/angle limitations, silhouette still understandable.
confidenceCriteria.low: Usable but limited evidence: loose clothing, partial angle, mirror distortion, or only one imperfect view.
